name: First United Bank And Trust Company
description_short: Full-service bank offering checking, savings, mortgages, business banking, and insurance products across Texas and Oklahoma with 125+ years of history.
description_long:
First United Bank and Trust Company is an established financial institution celebrating over 125 years of operation. The bank positions itself as a community-focused provider with a stated mission of faith and purpose, emphasizing long-term customer relationships and financial stewardship.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of consumer and business banking products. For individuals, this includes three checking account tiers (Prestige, Basic, and Secure Checking), savings accounts, money market accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), and high-yield savings options. On the lending side, they provide mortgage services for home purchases and refinancing, personal loans, and overdraft protection through their Overdraft Advantage program. Business customers can access dedicated business checking and savings accounts, plus merchant services through BancCard and FiTech platforms. The bank also offers investment and trust services including retirement planning, estate planning, investment management, and trust administration.

First United Bank distinguishes itself through its 125-year heritage and integrated insurance offerings, which include personal auto insurance, home insurance, commercial insurance, and employee benefits programs. They operate multiple physical locations across Texas and Oklahoma, with dedicated insurance locations and ATM networks. The bank maintains FDIC insurance backing and emphasizes security awareness through educational resources about fraud prevention.

As a traditional regional bank, First United Bank's primary limitation is geographic reach—serving primarily Texas and Oklahoma rather than offering nationwide digital-only banking. The website content focuses on product availability and security messaging rather than detailing specific rates, fees, or comparative advantages. Customers seeking high-tech fintech features or national branch networks may find better alternatives, though the bank's brick-and-mortar presence and comprehensive services appeal to established customers seeking full-service banking relationships.
services: Personal and Commercial Insurance (auto, home, commercial, employee benefits)
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by U.S. government full faith and credit
Integrated insurance services (auto, home, commercial, employee benefits) under one institution
Multiple checking account options tailored to different customer needs (Prestige, Basic, Secure)
Comprehensive business banking platform with merchant services (BancCard and FiTech)
Trust and estate planning services available alongside banking products
125+ years of operational history demonstrating stability
Active fraud prevention education and scam awareness messaging
cons: Geographic limitation to Texas and Oklahoma only—no nationwide or online-only option
Website lacks specific product rates, APYs, or fee schedules for comparison shopping
Limited digital-first features or fintech innovation emphasized compared to modern neobanks
No mention of mobile banking capabilities or app-based account management details
Sparse information about loan approval criteria, terms, or competitive advantages
best_for: Texas and Oklahoma residents seeking full-service banking with physical branch access
Small business owners needing integrated banking, merchant services, and payroll solutions
Customers wanting consolidated banking and insurance with one institution
Individuals planning estates or requiring trust administration services
diagnosis: First United Bank is best for regional customers in Texas and Oklahoma who value traditional full-service banking with integrated insurance, physical branch networks, and trust services. The main caveat is limited geographic availability and lack of competitive rate/fee transparency—customers should contact branches directly for current product terms and compare offerings with national banks.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
